s2.ptxt: Using SST, hold the vane pump assembly in a vise.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE POWER STEERING SUCTION PORT UNION

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt and power steering suction port union from the vane pump assembly.
Using a screwdriver, remove the O-ring from the power steering suction port union.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE POWER STEERING FLUID PRESSURE SWITCH

s2.ptxt: Remove the power steering fluid pressure switch from the vane pump assembly.

atten3.ptxt: Perform this procedure only when the power steering fluid pressure switch is replaced.

s2.ptxt: Remove the O-ring from the pressure port union.
Remove the flow control valve assembly and the flow control valve compression spring.
Remove the 4 bolts and vane pump rear housing from the vane pump front housing.
Using a screwdriver, remove the O-ring from the vane pump rear housing.

atten4.ptxt: Tape the screwdriver tip before use.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE VANE PUMP SHAFT WITH PULLEY

s2.ptxt: Using 2 screwdrivers, remove the vane pump shaft snap ring from the vane pump shaft with pulley.
Remove the vane pump shaft with pulley.
Remove the vane pump cam ring from the vane pump front housing.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE VANE PUMP FRONT SIDE PLATE

s2.ptxt: Remove the vane pump front side plate from the vane pump front housing.
Using a screwdriver, remove the O-ring from the vane pump front side plate.

atten4.ptxt: Tape the screwdriver tip before use.

s2.ptxt: Remove the O-ring from the vane pump front housing.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE VANE PUMP HOUSING OIL SEAL

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver and a shop rag or a piece of cloth, remove the vane pump housing oil seal.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the vane pump front housing.
